summary: Phase 10.1 (Deals Kanban + Detail + Timeline) verified end-to-end. Backend: 13/13 NEW timeline tests + 24/24 Phase-1 regression tests pass (37/37 total). Frontend: ADMIN /deals/board mounts with all 6 columns + counts + search + agent-filter, URL state syncs ?search=, cards clickable to /deals/:id, drag-and-drop fires PUT and persists status change; detail page mounts with title/amount/property/client/meta cards + timeline. AGENT RBAC honoured — agent-filter hidden, agent only sees own deals (1 of 1), and visiting another user's deal renders a clean 'You do not have access to this deal' error card with Back to deals button (no crash). open-deal-board-button on /deals header works and navigates to /deals/board.
critical_code_review_comments: Timeline endpoint correctly enforces RBAC (agent gets 403 on non-owned deal, 404 on missing deal, admin can read any).
Auto-logging is precise — cosmetic edits (title, expectedClosingDate) emit ZERO events; multi-field PUT emits exactly 3 (STATUS_CHANGED + AMOUNT_UPDATED + NOTES_UPDATED), no duplicates.
CREATED notes contain title, status, and formatted amount; actor.id = caller; items sorted newest-first; cap of 200 respected.
GET /api/deals/:id/activities is a true alias of /timeline — identical payload (id sequence equal).

Frontend Deal Board uses @dnd-kit (pointer events). Playwright must use a small initial activation move (≥8px) before the big drag move for dnd-kit's PointerSensor activationConstraint to engage — captured in the test script for future runs.

DealBoardPage `data-testid` selectors are well-placed: deal-board-page, deal-board-column-{STATUS}, deal-board-count-{STATUS}, deal-board-search-input, deal-board-agent-filter, deal-board-card-{id}. NOTE: cards-per-column count returned by the wrapper-level selector includes 2 extra duplicate matches per card (likely the dragOverlay + sortable element each carrying the same id testid). Cosmetic — does not affect UX. The visible 'X of Y deals' header counter is correct (2 of 2 / 1 of 1).
DealDetailPage has all spec testids: deal-detail-page, deal-detail-title, deal-detail-amount, deal-property-card, deal-client-card, deal-meta-card, deal-timeline, deal-timeline-item-{id}.
AGENT 403 deal route is rendered as a friendly empty-state with 'You do not have access to this deal' + 'Back to deals' link — exactly per spec, no crash.

success_rate.frontend: 100% on Phase 10.1 acceptance criteria exercised (board mount, 6 columns, counts, search, URL sync, card→detail nav, drag-and-drop PUT, agent RBAC hide agent-filter, agent 403 page, open-deal-board-button)

seed_data_creation: Each timeline test creates+deletes its own deal (TEST_TL_* prefix) via fixtures. Vivek - Bandra 3BHK was temporarily moved from NEGOTIATION→LOST by the Playwright drag test and then restored to NEGOTIATION via direct API PUT before finishing. As a side-effect, two extra STATUS_CHANGED entries now exist in Vivek's timeline (DOCUMENTATION→LOST and LOST→NEGOTIATION) — these are legitimate auto-log entries from real user actions, not stray data.
verified_flows: BACKEND GET /api/deals/:id/timeline — response shape {items:[{id, source:'DEAL', eventType, notes, createdAt, actor:{id,name}|null}]}, sorted newest-first, cap 200
BACKEND GET /api/deals/:id/activities is a true alias of /timeline (identical id sequence)
BACKEND admin can read any deal's timeline; agent gets 403 on non-owned deal; agent 200 on own deal
BACKEND POST /api/deals → exactly 1 CREATED event with title/status/formatted amount in notes, actor=caller
BACKEND PUT status-only → 1 STATUS_CHANGED; amount-only → 1 AMOUNT_UPDATED (with from/to in notes); agent-only (admin) → 1 AGENT_REASSIGNED; notes-only → 1 NOTES_UPDATED
BACKEND PUT multi-field (status+amount+notes) → exactly 3 events (no extra/missing)
BACKEND Phase-1 regression: 24/24 pytest still pass (CRUD + RBAC + Restrict relations + cross-module health)
FRONTEND ADMIN /deals header: open-deal-board-button visible + clickable → navigates to /deals/board; add-deal-button + deals-view-toggle still present

FRONTEND ADMIN /deals/board: deal-board-page mounts; 6 columns (NEW/NEGOTIATION/DOCUMENTATION/PAYMENT_PENDING/WON/LOST) with deal-board-count-{STATUS} chips; deal-board-search-input + deal-board-agent-filter render
FRONTEND URL state sync: typing in search updates ?search=… in URL
FRONTEND drag-and-drop: dragging Vivek card from DOCUMENTATION column fires exactly 1 PUT /api/deals/:id and card moves to target column; reload persists the new status
FRONTEND clicking a deal card navigates to /deals/:id detail page

FRONTEND ADMIN /deals/:id detail: deal-detail-page, deal-detail-title, deal-detail-amount, deal-property-card (with Open property), deal-client-card (with Open client + phone + email), deal-meta-card (with Recent status history), deal-timeline + deal-timeline-item-{id} all render
FRONTEND AGENT /deals/board: page mounts but agent-filter is NOT visible (admin-only); agent sees scoped list (1 of 1 deal)
FRONTEND AGENT visiting a deal they don't own: renders 'You do not have access to this deal' card + 'Back to deals' link; page does not crash
context_for_next_testing_agent: Phase 10.1 is fully verified. Two test files cover the Deals module: /app/backend/tests/test_deals.py (24 Phase-1 tests) and /app/backend/tests/test_deal_timeline.py (13 Phase 10.1 tests). Both must remain green going forward. Key testids on /deals/board: deal-board-page, deal-board-column-{STATUS}, deal-board-count-{STATUS}, deal-board-search-input, deal-board-agent-filter, deal-board-card-{id}. Key testids on /deals/:id: deal-detail-page, deal-detail-title, deal-detail-amount, deal-property-card, deal-client-card, deal-meta-card, deal-timeline, deal-timeline-item-{id}. For Playwright drag-and-drop against @dnd-kit's PointerSensor: do mouse.down(), then a small (~15px) initial mouse.move(steps=5), wait 300ms, then the long mouse.move(steps=30) to target, wait 500ms, mouse.up(). The agent account 'Test Agent' owns the TEST deal. The 'Vivek - Bandra 3BHK' deal is owned by Admin and lives in NEGOTIATION as of end of this iteration. Both deals share the same Client (TEST_UI_Client_1779223606) and Property (2 BHK, New Delhi) — these were created in prior smoke runs.
rca of the issue: No issues found in Phase 10.1. One first-attempt false negative during testing: a naive Playwright mouse drag (mouse.down → big mouse.move) did NOT trigger @dnd-kit's PointerSensor because the library requires the pointer to satisfy an activation distance (~8px) BEFORE the actual drag commences. Fixed in the test script by issuing a tiny activation move first; subsequent drag worked and produced the expected PUT + STATUS_CHANGED auto-log. This is a Playwright/test-harness concern, NOT a product bug.
